codeReview.summary: The worker investigated the feature request to align Go's imageClasses() property split with Python's BLOCK_CONTAINER_PROPERTIES. Through careful experimentation, the worker determined that applying Python's full BLOCK_CONTAINER_PROPERTIES partition to Go's imageClasses() causes regressions (Martyr 0→8 diffs, HG 45→53 diffs, 10→12 test failures) because Go's effectiveStyle() includes inherited properties from the KFX style chain that Python's content_style doesn't have at the point of partition. The existing 4-property split (margin-top, margin-bottom, text-align, text-indent on wrapper) already produces identical final style strings after simplifyStylesFull runs and strips heritable properties. The worker correctly reverted the attempted change and documented the analysis. The commit only adds a documentation file (.factory/library/image-split-analysis.md) — no code changes were needed or made.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The image-split-analysis.md document added to .factory/library/ contains important architectural knowledge: Go's effectiveStyle() includes inherited properties from the KFX style chain at imageClasses() time, while Python's content_style only has explicitly set properties. This timing difference means BLOCK_CONTAINER_PROPERTIES partition cannot be used directly in Go's imageClasses(). This is already captured in the library.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/image-split-analysis.md 'Why Full BLOCK_CONTAINER_PROPERTIES Partition Doesn't Work' section

sharedStateObservations.observation: The root cause of the 87 cosmetic class index diffs is encounter-order differences in fixupStylesAndClasses when styles have equal occurrence counts. This is documented in the analysis file and partially in AGENTS.md 'Cosmetic Diffs' section. The AGENTS.md cosmetic diffs section already covers this well.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/image-split-analysis.md 'Root Cause of Cosmetic Renumbering' section; AGENTS.md 'Cosmetic Diffs' section

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature was marked 'completed' despite making no code changes. This is legitimate — the worker proved the change would be counterproductive and documented why. However, the feature description in features.json still describes the original (now disproven) plan. The worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges notes this: 'Feature description should be updated to reflect that image property split is already correct.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0]; features.json description still references the original BLOCK_CONTAINER_PROPERTIES fix plan

summary: PASS — The worker correctly investigated the fix-image-property-split feature, attempted the BLOCK_CONTAINER_PROPERTIES partition, found it causes regressions (Martyr +8 diffs, HG +8 diffs, +2 test failures), reverted, and documented the analysis proving the current 4-property split already produces identical final style strings. No code changes were committed; only an analysis document was added to .factory/library/. The worker followed the go-port-worker skill procedure correctly: baseline checks, implementation attempt, multi-file verification, and revert when diffs increased. The root cause of the 87 cosmetic diffs is encounter-order differences in fixupStylesAndClasses, not image property splitting.
